mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate deterioration in household income and consumer spending capacity, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the following trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances following a >0.5pp monthly UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and capital demand, generating sustained equity appreciation over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and fading marginal productivity, prompting investors to discount near-term earnings and sell equities over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, providing immediate upward momentum to equity markets within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipated monetary tightening, causing investors to price in higher discount rates and drive negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration above 0.4% MoM is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns (win rate < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Contracting industrial output during a monetary tightening cycle signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced factory utilization, triggering rapid short-term equity liquidation within the next trading week.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.
